$comment: MCP tool contract for `check`. The verdict comes only from deterministic engine execution; the model classifies checkability and translates - it never decides. formal_form and engine_result make every verdict auditable (FR-007). not_checkable is a successful, honest outcome (FR-004).
name: check
description: Settle a checkable claim by execution, not judgment. The claim is translated into a small formal problem - an arithmetic comparison or a logic/constraint system - and a deterministic engine executes it: no judge to fool, no calibration, no sycophancy. Returns the verdict with the executed formal form and the engine's raw result so the check is auditable, plus a solver witness when one exists. Claims that cannot be formalized honestly (judgment, taste, open questions) return not_checkable with the reason - route those to verify instead.

claim.description: The claim to check, in natural language. Best for arithmetic, quantitative comparisons, and logical/constraint consistency.

context.description: Optional background the claim depends on (definitions, given values).

engine.description: Which engine executed the check; null iff not_checkable.

formal_form.description: The executed expression or constraint script - audit the translation without re-running it.

engine_result.description: The engine's raw result (evaluated value, or sat/unsat).

witness.description: Solver model when one exists: the satisfying assignment, or the counterexample refuting an impossibility claim.

explanation.description: Deterministic, server-assembled explanation tying the engine result to the claim.

reason.description: Why the claim is not checkable (verdict = not_checkable only).
